meta.title: Discover China | Top Travel Cities Guide
meta.description: Explore China's most popular destinations: Beijing, Shanghai, Xi'an, Chengdu, Guilin, and Hangzhou.

hero.title: Discover Millennia of Culture and Modern Wonders
hero.subtitle: From the Great Wall to the Bund, from the Terracotta Army to the Li River—explore China's most captivating cities on an unforgettable Eastern journey.

cities.sectionSubtitle: Six must-visit cities spanning history, nature, cuisine, and modern urban life

beijing.name: Beijing
beijing.desc: China's capital boasts UNESCO World Heritage sites including the Forbidden City, Temple of Heaven, and Summer Palace, plus the magnificent Great Wall. Hutong alleys blend old Beijing charm with modern vitality.

shanghai.name: Shanghai
shanghai.tagline: Pearl of the Orient · Global Metropolis
shanghai.desc: China's largest economic hub, where the Bund's colonial architecture faces Lujiazui's skyscrapers across the Huangpu River. Nanjing Road, Yu Garden, and Xintiandi showcase unique Haipai culture.

xian.name: Xi'an
xian.desc: A cradle of Chinese civilization, home to the Terracotta Army—often called the Eighth Wonder of the World. The city wall, Giant Wild Goose Pagoda, and Muslim Quarter span over two millennia of history.

chengdu.name: Chengdu
chengdu.desc: Famous for its laid-back lifestyle and the Giant Panda Research Base. Hot pot, Sichuan cuisine, Kuanzhai Alley, and Jinli Ancient Street offer endless delights.

guilin.name: Guilin
guilin.desc: Karst peaks along the Li River create postcard-perfect landscapes. Yangshuo's West Street buzzes with international flair, while Longji Rice Terraces and Silver Cave showcase nature's artistry.

hangzhou.name: Hangzhou
hangzhou.tagline: Paradise on Earth · West Lake Gem
hangzhou.desc: West Lake's Ten Scenes are legendary—Spring Dawn at Su Causeway, Melting Snow on Broken Bridge. Lingyin Temple, Longjing tea plantations, and the Southern Song Imperial Street enrich every visit.

tips.sectionSubtitle: Practical information for a smooth journey

visa.title: Visa Policy
visa.desc: Most nationalities require a visa in advance. Some cities offer 144-hour transit visa-free entry for citizens of 53 countries—check the latest policy before travel.

payment.title: Mobile Payment
payment.desc: WeChat Pay and Alipay are ubiquitous. Link an international credit card beforehand, or exchange some cash at airports and hotels as backup.

transport.title: High-Speed Rail
transport.desc: China's HSR network connects major cities efficiently. Book via 12306.cn or platforms like Trip.com and Fliggy—advance booking is recommended.

season.title: Best Seasons
season.desc: Spring (Mar–May) and autumn (Sep–Nov) offer pleasant weather for most cities. Summer requires sun protection; winter in the north is cold but offers snowy scenery.

about.desc: A static travel guide helping international visitors discover China's most popular cities. Content is for reference only—verify visa, transport, and attraction details before your trip.
about.disclaimer: Images from Unsplash (https://unsplash.com), under the Unsplash License.
